hierarchy.title: भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
hierarchy.text:
भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
www.rbi.org.in
RBI/DPSS/2026-27/396
RBI/CO.DPSS.POLC.No.S56/02.14.003/2026-27

 April 21, 2026
Index Introduction Short Title and Commencement Applicability Definitions Registration and revocation of E-mandate Processing of First Transaction and Subsequent Recurring Transactions Pre-Transaction Notification Post-Transaction Notification Transaction Limits and Velocity Check Dispute Resolution and Grievance Redressal Other Provisions Repeal
Digital Payments - E-mandate Framework, 2026
In exercise of the powers conferred by Sections 10(2) read with Section 18 of the
Payment and Settlement Systems (PSS) Act, 2007, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI),
being satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest so to do,
hereby consolidates all circulars pertaining to e-mandates and issues the Directions
hereinafter specified.

children.title: (a) These Directions shall be called the "Digital Payments - E-mandate
children.text:
(a) These Directions shall be called the "Digital Payments - E-mandate
Framework, 2026".

children.title: (b) These Directions shall be effective immediately.
children.text: (b) These Directions shall be effective immediately.

hierarchy.title: 2. Applicability
hierarchy.text:
2. Applicability
The provisions of these Directions shall be applicable to all Payment System
Providers and Payment System Participants in respect of processing of recurring
transactions, domestic or cross-border, using cards / PPI / UPI.

children.title: (a) The terms 'authentication', 'factor of authentication', 'issuer', 'merchant'
children.text:
(a) The terms 'authentication', 'factor of authentication', 'issuer', 'merchant'
will have the same meaning as defined in Reserve Bank of India
(Authentication mechanisms for digital payment transactions) Directions, 2025
dated September 25, 2025 and Master Direction on Regulation of Payment
Aggregator (PA) dated September 15, 2025.

children.title: (a) A customer desirous of opting for e-mandate facility shall undertake a one-time
children.text:
(a) A customer desirous of opting for e-mandate facility shall undertake a one-time
registration process. The mandate shall be registered only after successful
validation of additional factor of authentication (AFA), in addition to the normal
process required by the issuer.

children.title: (b) Every e-mandate registered by the issuer shall specify the validity period of the
children.text:
(b) Every e-mandate registered by the issuer shall specify the validity period of the
e-mandate. The issuer shall provide the customer with a facility to modify the
validity period or withdraw the e-mandate at any point of time. Information
about this facility shall be clearly communicated to the customer at the time of
registration.

children.title: (c) The e-mandate may be for either a pre-specified fixed amount or for a variable
children.text:
(c) The e-mandate may be for either a pre-specified fixed amount or for a variable
amount subject to the overall cap fixed by the RBI. In the case of variable e-
mandates, the issuer shall provide the customer with a facility to specify the
maximum value of any recurring transaction.

children.title: (d) The customer shall be given a facility to choose or change a mode among
children.text:
(d) The customer shall be given a facility to choose or change a mode among
available options (SMS, email, etc.) for receiving the pre-transaction
notification from the issuer.

children.title: (e) Any modification in, or withdrawal of, an existing e-mandate shall require AFA
children.text:
(e) Any modification in, or withdrawal of, an existing e-mandate shall require AFA
validation by the issuer.

hierarchy.title: 5. Processing of first transaction and subsequent recurring transactions
hierarchy.text: 5. Processing of first transaction and subsequent recurring transactions

children.title: (a) The first transaction under an e-mandate shall require AFA validation. If the
children.text:
(a) The first transaction under an e-mandate shall require AFA validation. If the
first transaction is processed along with registration of the e-mandate, then
AFA validation may be combined.

children.title: (b) Payments under e-mandates shall not be subject to any other limits / controls
children.text:
(b) Payments under e-mandates shall not be subject to any other limits / controls
set by the customer.

children.title: (a) An issuer shall send a pre-transaction notification to the customer, at least 24
children.text:
(a) An issuer shall send a pre-transaction notification to the customer, at least 24
hours prior to the actual charge / debit.

children.title: (b) The pre-transaction notification shall, at the minimum, inform the customer
children.text:
(b) The pre-transaction notification shall, at the minimum, inform the customer
about the merchant's name, transaction amount, date / time of debit, reference
number of e-mandate, reason for debit, i.e., e-mandate registered by the
customer.

children.title: (c) The issuer shall provider a customer with a facility to opt-out of any particular
children.text:
(c) The issuer shall provider a customer with a facility to opt-out of any particular
transaction or the e-mandate. Any such opt-out shall be validated by the issuer
using AFA. An intimation to this effect shall be sent to the customer.

children.title: (d) Pre-transaction notification is not required for e-mandates registered to auto-
children.text:
(d) Pre-transaction notification is not required for e-mandates registered to auto-
replenish balances of FASTag, and National Common Mobility Card (NCMC).

hierarchy.title: 7. Post-transaction notification
hierarchy.text:
7. Post-transaction notification
An issuer shall send a post-transaction notification to the customer. This
notification shall, at the minimum, inform the customer about the merchant's
name, transaction amount, date and time of debit, reference number of
transaction and e-mandate, reason for debit, i.e., e-mandate registered by the
customer, and details on grievance redressal1.

children.title: (a) All recurring transactions may be authorised without AFA up to ₹15,000/- per
children.text:
(a) All recurring transactions may be authorised without AFA up to ₹15,000/- per
transaction. Transactions above this amount shall be subject to AFA.

children.title: (b) Payment of insurance premiums, subscription to mutual funds, and credit card
children.text:
(b) Payment of insurance premiums, subscription to mutual funds, and credit card
bill payments may be made without AFA up to ₹1,00,000/- per transaction.

children.title: (a) An appropriate dispute redressal system shall be put in place by the issuer to
children.text:
(a) An appropriate dispute redressal system shall be put in place by the issuer to
facilitate the customer to lodge grievance/s.

children.title: (b) RBI instructions on limiting liability of customers for unauthorised transactions
children.text:
(b) RBI instructions on limiting liability of customers for unauthorised transactions
shall be applicable to recurring transactions under e-mandates as well.
1 The requirement for providing details of grievance redressal is added, based on feedback.

children.title: (a) No charges shall be levied to the customer for availing the e-mandate facility
children.text:
(a) No charges shall be levied to the customer for availing the e-mandate facility
for recurring transactions.

children.title: (b) In case of cards, existing e-mandate(s) can be mapped to reissued cards2.
children.text: (b) In case of cards, existing e-mandate(s) can be mapped to reissued cards2.

children.title: (c) An acquirer shall ensure compliance with these directions by merchants on-
children.text:
(c) An acquirer shall ensure compliance with these directions by merchants on-
boarded by them.

hierarchy.title: 11. Repeal
hierarchy.text:
11. Repeal
With the issue of these directions, the instructions/guidelines contained in the
following circulars, issued by the Reserve Bank stand repealed.
No Circular No.
Date Subject
1.
DPSS.CO.PD.No.447/02.14.003/2019- 20 August 21,
2019 Processing of e-mandate on cards for recurring transactions
January 10, 2020 Processing of e-mandate in Unified Payments Interface (UPI) for recurring transactions
2.
DPSS.CO.PD No.1324/02.23.001/2019-20
3.
DPSS.CO.PD No.754/02.14.003/2020- 21 December
04, 2020 Processing of e-mandates for recurring transactions
4.
CO.DPSS.POLC.No.S34/02-14- 003/2020 March 31,
2021 Framework for processing of e-mandates for recurring online transactions
October 08, 2021 RBI's framework for e- mandate based recurring transactions
5.
Clarification issued to IBA on e- mandate based recurring transactions
6.
CO.DPSS.POLC.No.S- 518/02.14.003/2022-23 June 16,
2022 Processing of e-mandates for recurring transactions
7.
CO.DPSS.POLC.No.S- 882/02.14.003/2023-24 December
12, 2023 Processing of e-mandates for recurring transactions
8.
CO.DPSS.POLC.No.S528/02-14- 003/2024-25 August 22,
2024 Processing of e-mandates for recurring transactions

2 This clause is added based on feedback.
